entry.title: Electronic Documents Through VERI*FACTU in Spain
entry.content:
(Scroll down for the English version)

Que es VERI*FACTU?

En julio de 2021, el gobierno español aprobó la 11/2021 que introduce un conjunto de medidas para prevenir y combatir el fraude fiscal. Una de estas medidas obliga tanto a las empresas desarrolladoras como a los usuarios de sistemas informáticos que soportan procesos de facturación a garantizar la integridad, conservación, accesibilidad, legibilidad, trazabilidad e inalterabilidad de los registros. Esto debe hacerse sin interpolaciones, omisiones o alteraciones, de las cuales no quede constancia adecuada en los sistemas mismos.

Los contribuyentes pueden cumplir este requisito utilizando una de las siguientes dos opciones:

  • Estar acogido al Suministro Inmediato de Información (SII), ya sea porque la empresa está obligada a ello o porque se ha acogido voluntariamente al SII.
  • Implementar un sistema informático de emisión de facturas verificables (VERI*FACTU).

Es importante aclarar que esta normativa no se refiere a la facturación electrónica per se, sino al envío de información fiscal específica a la Agencia Tributaria.

Las empresas deberán adaptarse a esta normativa antes de enero de 2026, mientras que los profesionales tendrán hasta julio de 2026.

Fuente: Agencia Estatal de Administracion Tributaria (AEAT)

Características Principales de un Sistema VERI*FACTU

  • Creación simultánea de un registro de factura al momento de emitir o cancelar una factura.
  • Cada registro contará con una huella única para evitar su manipulación.
  • Cada uno de los registros deberá estar encadenado cronológicamente.
  • Envío simultáneo a la Agencia Tributaria de los registros.
  • Las facturas en papel deben incluir un código QR para que el receptor pueda verificar con su móvil si la factura ha sido enviada a la AEAT.

Cómo Prepararse para Adaptarse a esta Normativa

Las compañías deben realizar un análisis sobre aquellas empresas de su organización que no estén actualmente acogidas al SII, y decidir si adoptarán el SII a partir de enero de 2026 o implementarán un sistema de emisión de facturas verificables.

Si optan por un sistema VERI*FACTU, pueden considerar la solución basada en SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Cloud Edition, que garantiza el cumplimiento de la normativa para soluciones SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP S/4HANA y SAP ERP mediante las siguientes capacidades:

  • Generar registros de factura estructurados conforme a la normativa
  • Automatizar el proceso de envío de registros a la AEAT
  • Incluir los códigos QR en la impresión de facturas
  • Monitor detallado de la cadena de registros creados, enviados y con su estado

Diagrama de flujo de datos

Para detalles técnicos de la solución VERI*FACTU revisar la nota: 3585725 - Electronic Document Processing for Spain VERI*FACTU: Implementation Overview

Para un resumen de los puntos más relevantes de esta normativa, consulta el KBA; 3609812 - FAQ - Electronic Document Processing for Spain VERI*FACTU legal change 2025/2026

Para los próximos cambios legales relativos a VERI*FACTU, la recomendación es utilizar nuestro Regulatory Change Manager, donde puede encontrar el estado más reciente de esta normativa.

También existe un Workzone dedicado a España: Spain - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, donde puede compartir, comentar y debatir cualquier tema relacionado con documentos electrónicos.

***

What is VERI*FACTU?

In July 2021, the Spanish government approved the   11/2021 introducing a set of measures to prevent and combat tax fraud. One of these measures requires both software providers companies and users of computer systems supporting invoicing processes to ensure the integrity, storage, accessibility, readability, traceability, and immutability of records, without interpolations, omissions, or alterations that are not adequately documented in the systems themselves.

Taxpayers can meet this requirement by using one of the following two options:

  • Being subscribed to the Immediate Supply of Information (SII), either because the company is obliged to do so or has voluntarily adopted the SII.
  • Implementing a computer system for issuing verifiable invoices (VERI*FACTU).

It is important to clarify that this regulation does not refer to electronic invoicing per se but to the transmission of specific fiscal information to the Tax Agency.

Companies must adapt to this regulation by January 2026, while professionals can do it until July 2026.

Source: State Tax Administration Agency (AEAT)

Key Features of a VERI*FACTU System

  • Simultaneous creation of an invoice record when issuing or canceling an invoice.
  • Each record will have a unique footprint to prevent manipulation.
  • Each record must be chained chronologically.
  • Simultaneous transmission of records to the Tax Agency.
  • Paper invoices must include a QR code so the recipient can verify with their mobile device whether the invoice has been sent to the AEAT.

How to Prepare for Adapting to This Regulation

 

Companies need to analyze those entities within their organization that are not currently subscribed to the SII, and decide whether they will adopt the SII starting January 2026 or implement a system for issuing verifiable invoices.

If they opt for a VERI*FACTU system, they can consider the solution based on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Cloud Edition, which ensures compliance with regulations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP ERP solutions through the following capabilities:

  • Generate structured invoice records in accordance with regulations
  • Automate the process of sending records to the AEAT
  • Include QR codes in invoice printing

Detailed monitoring of the chain of records created, sent, and their status

Data flow diagram

For technical details of the VERI*FACTU solution, review the note: 3585725 - Electronic Document Processing for Spain VERI*FACTU: Implementation Overview.

For a summary of the most relevant points of this regulation, refer to KBA: 3609812 - FAQ - Electronic Document Processing for Spain VERI*FACTU legal change 2025/2026.

For upcoming legal changes of VERI*FACTU, the recommendation is to use our Regulatory Change Manager, where you can find the latest status of this regulation.

There is also a Workzone dedicated for Spain - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance where you can check, comment and discuss any topic related to electronic documents.

entry.title: SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria
entry.content:
Nigeria’s Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) has launched its Merchant Buyer Solution (MBS)—a PEPPOL-aligned electronic invoicing platform set to revolutionize tax compliance. Rollout begins with large taxpayers in July 2025, extending to medium and small businesses soon after.

We are working directly with the FIRS team on our SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria Solution which is discussed below:

How It Works

  • ERP or API Integration: Businesses can integrate their existing Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems with the MBS via API connectors or middleware plugins. This integration ensures seamless transmission of invoices from back-end systems directly to the FIRS validation hub.
  • Invoice Generation & Validation:
    • For B2B/B2G transactions, invoices are submitted to FIRS before being shared with the buyer. The invoice undergoes validation and receives a unique IRN, CSID, and a QR codethat ensures authenticity and traceability.
    • For B2C invoices, the supplier issues the invoice immediately to the customer and must upload it to the MBS within 24 hours for reporting compliance.
    • Storage & Auditability: All validated invoices are stored in the MBS repository, where both suppliers and buyers, as well as the FIRS, can retrieve them for audit, reconciliation, or VAT verification. 

FIRS has given a number of use cases which leads to solution options for clients :

Core Use Cases & Capabilities

  • B2B / B2G Invoicing (Continuous Transaction Controls): This use case supports digital exchange of invoices between businesses or government agencies. Each invoice must be validated in real-time by the FIRS Merchant Buyer Solution (MBS) system, receiving a unique Invoice Reference Number (IRN), a Cryptographic Stamp (CSID), and a QR code for authentication.

In the above scenario's a business can choose the following:

1) Register their ERP as an access point and connect direct to the FIRS system; while this is possible during our discussions with FIRS this is not the prefered solution

2) Use a certified access point and connect your ERP to this as with other PEPPOL solution, one of FIRS goals is also to understand cross border invoice flows and by connecting to the PEPPOL network FIRS will then be reported on overseas customer invoices and overseas vendor invoices recieved via the access point.

  • Crimson-Partners Access Point is already registered in Nigeria as per our other solutions and can provide this for you
  • A company can choose their own access point and our solution will connect to this

B2C Invoicing (Near‑real‑time Reporting): Retail transactions involving consumers must be reported within 24 hours. These invoices are issued directly to customers and subsequently uploaded to the MBS platform for compliance.

  • Working with FIRS we have discussed using a consolidated invoice process daily to achieve this reporting for those with B2C sales

  • Standardized Formats & Access Points: Nigeria has adopted the UBL (Universal Business Language) XML standard to ensure interoperability. Invoices must be transmitted through licensed Access Point Providers (APPs) certified by NITDA. These APPs provide secure and traceable communication channels between senders and receivers via the PEPPOL 4-corner model. 

FIRS also provides an API to check the TIN validity of your customer before submitting invoicing accurately

✅Benefits for Nigerian Organizations

  • Improved Compliance: Real-time invoice validation eliminates the risks of underreporting or delayed VAT submissions.
  • Enhanced Transparency: The use of QR codes and digital cryptographic stamps ensures each invoice is traceable, tamper-proof, and verifiable by both buyer and tax authority.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automation reduces manual processing, lowers administrative overhead, and facilitates faster transaction processing.
  • Accurate VAT Reporting: Invoice data is automatically captured and structured for VAT compliance, reducing the need for manual declarations.

⚖ Legal Mandate & Penalties
The implementation of electronic invoicing is legally mandated by the FIRS under the Finance Act, supported by regulatory frameworks from NITDA and NCC. Non-compliance can lead to penalties including monetary fines, interest on underreported taxes, and restrictions on eligibility for input VAT claims.

� Rollout Strategy & Timeline

  • July 2025: Mandatory for large taxpayers as classified by FIRS.
  • Q4 2025: Gradual onboarding of medium-sized businesses.
  • 2026: Full rollout to small businesses and B2C operations.

Crimson provide a standard SAP Document Reporting Compliance Solution, a full staging area for testing, training and handbooks for your organisation

Benefits :

  • Single Global Process using Document Reporting Compliance
  • E2E compliant solution built with the FIRS team
  • Generation of electronic invoices in XML format (FI, SD) for B2B and B2G
  • Self-billing scenario's
  • FICA and RE-FX billing
  • Manual or automated invoice submission
  • Receiving of e-invoices in XML format and Integration to VIM Software
  • Human Readable PDF in Cockpit for incoming invoices

� Next Steps

  • Confirm your registration with FIRS and verify your Tax Identification Number (TIN).
  • Engage with a licensed Access Point Provider (APP) or Software Integrator (SI) to assist with system integration such as Crimson who can meet both these
  • Prepare your ERP systems or point-of-sale platforms for API connectivity and XML invoice generation.
  • Conduct end-to-end testing with FIRS and ensure your teams are trained in the e-invoicing process and legal requirements. 

� Bottom Line
The FIRSMBS e-invoicing system marks a major milestone in Nigeria’s digital tax transformation. It also marks a shift for African countries with other African nations choosing highly complex solutions, Nigeria has chosen a global standard and is following in similar fashion to Malaysia, UAE and other upcoming mandates.

It ensures:

  • Real-time transparency
  • Reduced invoicing errors
  • Automated VAT reporting
  • Lower compliance costs

Compatible with ECC, S/4HANA & S/4 Cloud, this is another step on the journey of global electronic invoicing across borders.

Look out next for our Kenya, Zambia, Ghana solution and BTP Application!

Crimson Tax Tech | FIRS e-Invoicing Advisory

entry.title: New Danish Tax requirements in 2025 and beyond - How SAP can help
entry.content:
Marc Hoessels (PwC Belgium - Managing Director Tax & ERP Transformations) and Anders Strandet Jepsen (PwC Denmark - Partner)

 

  1. Introduction to the Danish Bookkeeping Act

The Danish Bookkeeping Act, in Danish “Bogføringsloven”, outlines the principles and requirements for digital accounting and bookkeeping in Denmark. It includes several immediate tax-related obligations for businesses, such as:

  • Supporting the automatic sending and receiving of e-invoices in OIOUBL/PEPPOL BIS 
  • Enabling the sharing of bookkeeping data in SAF-T format.

Beyond these tax-related requirements, the Act imposes broader bookkeeping obligations, including but not limited to:

  • Performing necessary tax reconciliations for each tax-relevant filing.
  • Registering all company transactions in compliance with bookkeeping standards.
  • Maintaining an audit trail for recorded transactions.
  • Digitally storing purchase and sales invoices.
  • Conducting a full backup of stored data weekly with an independent party inside the EU that meets IT security standards.

These regulations aim to enhance financial transparency, data security, and compliance within Denmark’s digital bookkeeping framework.

Further, the Act distinguishes between approved and non-approved digital bookkeeping systems, where the requirements can differ and the responsibility to comply can lie with either the company or the ERP provider. For non-approved systems, the responsibility lies with the company.

It is therefore important to determine which category one's bookkeeping system belongs to, and which associated requirements must be met and how to meet them. As this could have an impact on when the requirements are/become applicable (depending on the financial year of the entity):

  • 1 July 2024 (past): Digital bookkeeping applies for approved ERP systems
  • 1 January 2025 (present): Digital bookkeeping applies for non-approved ERP systems for most companies in Denmark
  • 1 January 2026 (future): Digital bookkeeping applies to all entities with net annual turnover over DKK 300,000 (approx. EUR 40,000), including foreign companies with branches in Denmark

SAP has clarified that SAP S/4HANA is not officially recognized as a standard bookkeeping system under the Danish Bookkeeping Act. Therefore, it is the client's responsibility to ensure that they are in compliance with the Danish Bookkeeping Act. To help you with this PwC can perform a ‘compliance validation’. This process helps you identify and address any compliance gaps related to the Danish Bookkeeping Act, which encompasses more than just VAT requirements (such as SAF-T, e-invoicing, etc.). As it also includes requirements related to:

  • Ensuring a control trail for accounting data
  • Availability of banking data
  • Reconciliation of banking data
  • Regular back ups of data
  • …

2. E-Invoice compliance under the Act

As mentioned, companies must be able to automatically send and receive e-invoices in Peppol and OIOUBL format. OIOUBL is a Danish e-invoicing standard maintained by the Danish Business Authority and is typically used for transactions with Danish government entities via the NemHandel platform.

Peppol, another accepted e-invoicing format under the Danish Bookkeeping Act, is a globally recognized standard. To use Peppol, companies need a certified Peppol access point before engaging in Peppol e-invoicing. Additionally, it's crucial to ensure that your customers are registered on the Peppol network; otherwise, your e-invoices may not reach their intended destination.

The requirements in the Danish Bookkeeping Act outlines the requirements to system capabilities. The Danish Tax Agency has not (yet) mandated the use for VAT taxable B2B or B2C transactions, but the legislations are expected to align in the near future (likely to be introduced in 2028). 

Next to the strict Danish obligation, also ViDA is around the corner (2030), which impacts your EU cross border transactions with the EU wide implementation of Digital Reporting Requirements (DRR). One aspect of the DRR is to move cross border trade closer to real-time reporting by abolishing the periodic recapitulative statements (e.g. EU sales listings) and instead mandating the suppliers to report the cross border trade within 10 days of the chargeable event. This coincides with the e-invoicing requirements, that are also part of the DRR, where e-invoices is the new default for invoicing and e-invoices must be issued within the same time frame. 

Since more and more countries have implemented, will implement or plan to implement a kind of e-invoicing, it is a good business decision to look at all obligations in the EU and beyond and try to harmonise your e-invoicing solution(s) which will lower your IT cost & maintenance over time. 

3. SAF-T (Standard Audit File for Tax)

The new Bookkeeping Act requires bookkeeping systems to support the SAF-T international standard file format. This facilitates easier and more secure data exchanges between companies, such as with the Danish Tax Agency, and simplifies switching bookkeeping systems.


Currently, companies are not mandated to regularly submit SAF-T files to authorities. However, bookkeeping systems must be capable of generating, loading, and exporting SAF-T files if requested by the Danish Tax Agency, such as during a VAT or tax inspection. The current obligation requires businesses to produce a SAF-T file on header level.

However, this requirement is likely to be extended to all transactions (purchases and sales) by 2028 and with further reporting requirements in the subsequent years.

4. Implementation in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (hereafter SAP DRC) for DK e-invoicing and SAF-T

SAP offers a solution called SAP Document and Reporting Compliance to assist with indirect tax and e-invoicing reporting obligations globally. 

In Denmark, SAP DRC can generate from a reporting perspective:

  • VAT return (where Electronic submission of the VAT return is on the roadmap for delivery)
  • EC Sales Listings
  • The header details of the Danish SAF-T (SAP intends to deliver full SAF-T, once more root elements become mandatory).

From an e-reporting/e-invoicing perspective:

  • Both Peppol and OIOUBL formats via the Peppol Network
  • Peppol e-order
  • Integration to Nemhandel (on the roadmap for delivery).

entry.title: GST India: Input Service Distribution solution
entry.content:
“Input Service Distributor” means an office of the supplier of goods or services or both which receives tax invoices towards the receipt of input services, including invoices in respect of services liable to tax under sub-section (3) or sub-section (4) of section 9, for or on behalf of distinct persons referred to in section 25, and liable to distribute the input tax credit in respect of such invoices in the manner provided in section 20;

The Government of India, on August 6, 2024, declared that the Input Service Distributor (ISD) mechanism will be made mandatory effective April 1, 2025.

Additionally, the Finance Act, 2024, along with provisions in the Finance Bill, 2025, expanded the scope of ISD to include Input Tax Credit (ITC) on services subject to Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM). Accordingly, ITC on such RCM services must now be distributed exclusively through the ISD framework.

SAP has delivered solution for ISD functionality. It can handle below scenarios.

  • Distribution of credit from ISD to receiving Business places when vendor invoice is posted at ISD BUPLA
  • Transfer of credit from non-ISD to ISD BUPLA when vendor invoice is posted at non-ISD BUPLA and then distribute the credit from ISD to receiving Business places
    Note: RCM scenario should be handled via transfer of credit method
  • Printing of ISD distribution and credit transfer invoices
  • Reporting of ISD invoice

Key requirements

  • ISD must have a distinct GST registration, separate from regular branch GSTINs even if located at the same address.
  • All recipient GSTINs must belong to the same legal entity (under one PAN).
  • ITC distribution applies only to common input services (e.g. legal ) used across multiple units.
  • Credit must be allocated proportionally to each unit based on turnover.
  • ISD shall separately distribute the amount of ineligible input tax and the amount of eligible input tax credit.
  • Credit distribution must be documented via ISD invoices and reported monthly in Form GSTR-6.
  • No further re-distribution allowed, branches receiving ISD credit cannot re-distribute it to others.
  • Distribution formula:
    C1 = (T1 / T ) * C
    C = Amount of credit to be distributed
    T1 = Turnover in a state of a recipient during the relevant period
    T = Aggregate of turnover during the relevant period of all recipients to whom the common credit is attributable

Notes required for ISD solution:

GST India: ISD Solution : This guided answer contains all the notes related to SAP ISD solution

Configurations

Note: SSCUIs are only applicable for Public Cloud Edition

  1. Map the ISD business place to the receiving business places:

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Map ISD Business Places to Receiving Business Places.

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_ISD_MAP_V, SSCUI - 101911
  2. Maintain Turnover for Receiving Business Places: (Fiscal year will be picked based on Company code fiscal year variant)

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Maintain Turnover for Receiving Business Places

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_ISD_TN_V, SSCUI - 101912
  3. Map the tax G/L accounts to receiving business places (Maintain dummy tax code)

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Configure ISD Invoice Posting Settings

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_ISD_POST_V, SSCUI - 101927
  4. Maintain credit transfer configurations, go through KBA 3573453 for more details on the fields required here.

    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Maintain Tax Credit Transfer Configuration.

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J1IG_CT_CONFIG_V, SSCUI - 107062
  5. Maintain document types for tax credit transfer and distribution invoices
    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > Input Service Distributor > Specify Document Types for Goods and Services Tax for India

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_DOCTYP_V, SSCUI - 101910
  6. Maintain default tax code (dummy) for Credit transfer invoices
    SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings (New) > Tax on Sales/Purchases > Basic Settings > India > Goods and Services Tax > General Settings > Maintain Default Tax Code for Process Type

    Or, Go to tcode – SM30, View - J_1IG_PRCTXCODEV, SSCUI - 103810

Process

Credit transfer:

  1. Vendor invoice posted for non-ISD BUPLA – BEL1(Vendor invoice can be posted using MIRO/ FB60).
  2. Post credit transfer invoices using tcode - J1IG_ISDN:
    Note: The document number entered in all the options will be accounting document number except for “Display invoice distribution” and “Post invoice distribution” option. Here, you will be entering the invoice number in case of MIRO documents or accounting document number for FB60 documents.

    Create Customer invoice followed by supplier invoice.




    Customer invoice:

    Supplier Invoice:
  3. Display tax credit transfer details. (If the tax credit transfer is incomplete, you must at least create the customer invoice to ensure the accounting document is available when displaying the tax credit transfer)

    Once supplier invoice is created, distribution of credit can be done using the supplier invoice created via tcode – J1IG_ISDN.

Distribution of Credit from ISD to receiving Business places:

Distribution can be done directly if vendor invoice is posted at ISD Business Place.

  1. Display credit distribution:

    Distribution is done to all 4 receiving Business places.

  2. Recreate account distribution: This option can be used if credit needs to be redistributed to specific receiving Business places

    Credit is redistributed to entered receiving Business places only.

  3. Post Invoice distribution:

    Post distribution invoices using "Summarize" button, if there is any error while posting distribution invoice, it will be captured in logs which can be viewed using "log" button.

    Distribution invoice posted for receiving Business places

Reporting

Every ISD must file the following return forms:

  1. Form GSTR-6A: Contains details of all inward supplies made available to the ISD recipient based on GSTR-1 form furnished by the supplier.
  2. Form GSTR-6: Contains details of the tax invoices on which credit has been received i.e. distribution invoices.

SAP has provided Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition on SAP Business Technology Platform to meet compliance requirements. Refer GSTR 6 Report | SAP Help Portal for more details to meet ISD reporting compliance.

Printing of ISD invoices

Tcode - J_1HKORDIN can be used with standard/ Custom form templates for printing of ISD distribution and credit transfer invoices

ISD forms for S4 Core 107 and above releases:

  1. 3605786 - GST India: DDIC Corrections for ISD and Credit Transfer Print Form
  2. 3608554 - GST India: ISD and Credit Transfer Print Forms changes

NOTE: This section will be updated with Consulting notes for lower releases, once notes are released.

Public cloud: Print ISD Forms | SAP Help Portal

Troubleshooting and Support content

  • Input Service Distributor | SAP Help Portal - OP
  • Input Service Distribution | SAP Help Portal - Public cloud
  • GST India: ISD Solution
  • 3585077 - FAQ - Input Service Distribution (ISD) : GST IN - SAP for Me
  • ISD Webinar

NOTE: If you encounter any problems related to ISD functionality, we highly recommend you to first go through the links above for common issues and frequently asked questions. If your issue persists, please submit a ticket using the FI-LOC-MM-IN/FI-LOC-LO-IN component.
